rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAMONA LOPEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the attorney's (not the fiduciary's [CRC 7.103(c) and Probate Code Section 1023]) supplement filed on 2/25/25 did not sufficiently address how the payments out of escrow to the administrator and Ric Lopez were "reimbursements," when the administrator did not disclose who the original payees were or what service those payees provided. The attorney for this administrator also represents the administrator of the estate of Roberto Lopez, but nothing appears to have been filed to confirm that the heirs have waived the potential conflict. There is no need to "declare" in a pleading that a completed proof of service was filed; a proof of service should just be filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUSSELL J. BACOSA
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/24/25. (No earlier availability) (1) A Court-appointed fiduciary must sign and verify her own pleadings. [CRC 7.103(c) and Probate Code Section 1023.] (2) A certified copy of the letters issued within 60 days to the personal representative of the deceased heir's estate must be filed. (3) Notice must be given to (or waived by) the personal representative of deceased heir's estate (once he is appointed). (4) No Allowance or Rejection of the creditor's claim appears to have been filed yet. [Probate Code Section 9250(b).] Deadline for providing the examiner courtesy copies is 6/10/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WENTZ FAMILY LIVING TRUST DATED MARCH 14, 2011
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/13/25 per stipulation of Counsel and to be heard by the judicial officer assigned to the matter. (1) A "supplement" filed on 4/21/25 actually amended the accounting petition that was filed on 1/17/25, replacing entirely one of the accountings attached to the original petition. An amending pleading (by any name) requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] Unless all interested parties waive notice, the accounting petition is not procedurally ready to be heard. (2) The petition caption is incomplete. [CRC 7.102.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JEFFREY M. BRADFORD LVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/11/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A copy of the trust was not attached to the petition for the Court to be able to determine who is entitled to notice. (2) It does not appear that the petitioner signed/verified the petition. [Probate Code Sections 1020 and 1021.] (3) If the trust is not being administered in this county, this would not be the proper venue. When a court retains jurisdiction over a settlement agreement, that means the Superior Court of California; venue is where the trustee administers the trust. If this is the proper venue, please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any other supporting papers to the assigned examiner by 5/28/25. (No courtesy copies have been provided, so the petition has only been cursorily reviewed online. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.]) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE THOMAS & SHARON BLEAN TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the judicial officer to review and determine. While the subject Bank of America account titled in the deceased spouse-settlor's name was listed on the schedule of assets attached to the trust and existed when the trust and schedule of assets were signed, the subject USAA account titled in the deceased spouse-settlor's name was not listed on the schedule of trust assets and did not exist when the trust and pour-over will were signed. Moreover, "Assignment of Personal Property" only transferred tangible personal property and specifically excluded "cash and other intangible property" presumably including financial accounts other than those listed on the schedule of trust assets. See also Probate Code section 13501(b) requiring probate administration of "property disposed of in trust under the decedent's will". If the USAA account cannot be confirmed as an asset of the trust, the Court may discuss with counsel whether petitioner intends to amend this petition (or file a new spousal property petition) to request an order confirming 50% of the USAA account balance as belonging to petitioner as his 50% share of community property, followed by presentation of a small estate affidavit to USAA to collect the remaining account balance (which appears to be less than the applicable $184,500 small estate limit) as the deceased spouse's 50% share of community property distributable to petitioner as trustee of the devisee trust. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
